skill.name: web-styling
skill.description: Styling patterns for React web applications. Use when working with Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, theming, responsive design, or component styling.
skill.summary: Apply practical React styling patterns for Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, theming, responsive layouts, and component variants.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples, CSS color tokens, and layout text in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network access, environment access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside Markdown code fences that show React, Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, styled-components, Framer Motion, and layout examples. They are not executable skill scripts and do not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a single Markdown document, and the flagged lines are fenced examples or prose. No shell command, process API, install script, or executable helper file is present.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections correspond to documentation text, Markdown syntax, CSS class examples, or hexadecimal color values. No cryptographic API, hashing routine, password handling, or token generation logic appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas describe styling topics and color tokens rather than cryptographic algorithms. Searches found no crypto, hash, MD5, SHA1, or credential-handling code.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections occur in responsive grid and layout examples. The text does not inspect the host, enumerate files, read environment data, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are UI layout examples using grid and breakpoint terms. There is no code path that queries operating system, network, filesystem, or process metadata.

content.value_statement: React teams need consistent styling patterns that work across components and breakpoints. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, themes, variants, and layouts.
actual_capabilities: Provides Tailwind CSS examples for buttons, cards, responsive grids, dark mode, and custom design tokens.
Shows CSS Modules patterns for scoped component styles and conditional class composition.
Explains styled-components usage with variants and theme-driven values.
Documents component variant patterns using class-variance-authority style APIs.
Covers animation examples with Tailwind utilities and Framer Motion modal transitions.
Includes layout, theming, common issue, and file structure references for React projects.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, generators, or automated code migration tools.
Focuses on React web applications and may not fit native mobile or server-rendered-only projects.
Provides patterns and examples, not a complete design system specification.
Does not validate accessibility, browser support, or visual regressions automatically.

use_cases.title: Standardize React Component Styling
use_cases.description: Create consistent button, card, and variant patterns across a React interface.

use_cases.title: Plan Responsive Layouts
use_cases.description: Apply mobile-first Tailwind grid, flexbox, and container patterns to product screens.

use_cases.title: Improve Theme Consistency
use_cases.description: Document colors, spacing, dark mode, and CSS variable usage for a shared UI system.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Styled Button
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-styling skill to design a React button with Tailwind CSS variants for primary, secondary, and danger states.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Styles to CSS Modules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-styling skill to convert this React component from inline classes to CSS Modules with conditional class handling.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Responsive Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-styling skill to create a mobile-first dashboard layout with sidebar, main content, cards, and adaptive grid behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Define a Themed Component System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-styling skill to propose a React theming approach with tokens, dark mode, component variants, and animation patterns.

output_examples.input: Style a React card component for active and disabled states.
output_examples.output: A concise component styling plan with shared base classes, state-specific variants, and guidance for safe class merging.

output_examples.input: Create a responsive product grid using Tailwind CSS.
output_examples.output: A mobile-first layout recommendation with breakpoint behavior, column counts, spacing rules, and overflow prevention notes.

output_examples.input: Add dark mode support to a React interface.
output_examples.output: A theme strategy using class-based dark mode, color tokens, CSS variables, and notes for reducing initial render flicker.

best_practices: Start with shared design tokens before writing individual component styles.
Use mobile-first responsive classes and test the smallest supported viewport early.
Keep variants explicit so component states remain predictable and easy to review.
anti_patterns: Do not mix many unrelated styling approaches in one component without a migration reason.
Do not rely on arbitrary values when a shared token should define the decision.
Do not add animation without checking motion preference and interaction clarity.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown reference with styling examples and does not include scripts or executable helpers.

faq.question: Which styling tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, styled-components, class-variance-authority patterns, and Framer Motion examples.

faq.question: Can it help with dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes class-based dark mode examples, theme tokens, and CSS variable patterns.

faq.question: Is it only for React projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples and component patterns are written for React web applications.

faq.question: Does it create a complete design system?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns that can support a design system, but it is not a full specification.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it during implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill gives practical examples that Claude Code and Codex can adapt while editing React styling.
